title: Human Gates, Permissions, and Reversible Autonomy · Goal Brief: Approval Design
summary: Set human review points for a consequential operations agent. Planned artifact: Add and verify the loops approval design increment in a risk-tiered approval matrix. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Place approvals immediately before consequential effects with enough evidence to decide.
Classify actions by reversibility, blast radius, sensitivity, and external impact.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""Bounded agent-loop portfolio. No external effects occur in this browser lab."""

steps.title: Predict Approval Design
steps.instruction:
Use a changed theory case derived from this module: loops human control. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

content.text: Place approvals immediately before consequential effects with enough evidence to decide. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Check preconditions, perform the operation in an inspectable order, then verify the result and a boundary case.

content.title: Worked case pattern

steps.title: Defend the Approval Design model
